title: storage — model relationships
description: Four owned entities with tight internal graph. External graph is intentionally minimal: the module publishes the HasFiles trait so downstream models attach polymorphically (fileable_type / fileable_id) without adding foreign keys back to this module.

internal.description: Cascade on File hard-delete so variants never orphan. On SoftDelete, variants stay linked (they're regeneratable from the archived File).
Cascade on File erasure so the audit trail doesn't reference an erased byte.
Set null on File erasure so the ChunkedUpload audit row stays but stops referencing the (now-gone) file. State field remains `completed` for historical inspection.

outbound.description: Every file is tenant-scoped. Cascade on tenant erasure triggers the storage cascade job which decrements refcounts on the shared blob store.
Nullable owner — system uploads have no owner. Set null on user deletion so the file survives the user's departure (tenant still owns it).
Polymorphic parent. Nullable — files can exist without a parent (tenant-level attachments, admin uploads). The parent's HasFiles trait establishes the inverse morphMany.
Denormalised for tenant-scope queries. Kept in sync with File.tenant_id by the FileObserver.
Nullable — signed URLs for anonymous share links have no target user.
In-flight uploads deleted on owner removal — no way for another user to claim the abandoned upload.

inbound.description: Every consumer module attaches via HasFiles trait. NO consumer defines a foreign key back to `files.id` — the polymorphic morphMany on the parent side + morphTo on the file side is the sole mechanism. Consumer schemas MUST NOT add a `file_id` column; instead compose HasFiles + declare #[Attachable] on a property.
consumer_examples: invitations::Invitation + #[Attachable(kind: 'invitation_attachment')] for extra docs shipped with an invite
compliance::DsarExport + #[Attachable(kind: 'export_artifact')] for the DSAR zip archive

consumed.usage: Every upload calls entitlements::consume() with the file byte count against `storage.bytes.consumed`. Overquota rejects at middleware.
File erasures + admin quarantines land in the compliance-grade audit log via HasAuditable.
Uploads + downloads + shares land in the tenant activity feed via HasActivityLog.
